page: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the arm64-based AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V2 model, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. While single-core operations, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall within the average tier, multi-core execution is highly efficient. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core testing, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Memory subsystem performance is a notable strength, with both large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks ranking in the top tier. Conversely, LLM inference tasks show mixed results; text generation using a small model is weak, whereas text generation with a medium model registers as strong.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8g.12xlarge leverages custom ARM architecture to deliver high multi-threaded throughput and memory bandwidth relative to its resource footprint. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose applications that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is an arm64-based general-purpose server instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance but top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing, software compilation, and memory bandwidth. While LLM text generation for small models is weak, the instance demonstrates strong multi-threaded decompression and database operations. This hardware profile is suited for memory-bandwidth-sensitive applications, multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded workloads.

Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU hardware included in the configuration
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications
